question: Which patients have a total invoiced amount above the average total per patient?
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT a.patient_id AS ek, SUM(i.total_amount) AS total FROM invoices i JOIN appointments a ON i.appointment_id = a.appointment_id GROUP BY a.patient_id) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: First total the invoiced amount per patient; list patients above the average of those totals.
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT a.patient_id AS ek, SUM(i.total_amount) AS total FROM invoices i JOIN appointments a ON i.appointment_id = a.appointment_id GROUP BY a.patient_id) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Find patients whose overall invoiced amount exceeds the average patient total.
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT a.patient_id AS ek, SUM(i.total_amount) AS total FROM invoices i JOIN appointments a ON i.appointment_id = a.appointment_id GROUP BY a.patient_id) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Show each patient and its total where the total invoiced amount is above the cross-patient average.
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT a.patient_id AS ek, SUM(i.total_amount) AS total FROM invoices i JOIN appointments a ON i.appointment_id = a.appointment_id GROUP BY a.patient_id) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Which customers have a total purchase value above the average total per customer?
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT o.customer_id AS ek, SUM(oi.quantity * pr.unit_price) AS total FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN products pr ON oi.product_id = pr.product_id GROUP BY o.customer_id) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: First total the purchase value per customer; list customers above the average of those totals.
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT o.customer_id AS ek, SUM(oi.quantity * pr.unit_price) AS total FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN products pr ON oi.product_id = pr.product_id GROUP BY o.customer_id) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Find customers whose overall purchase value exceeds the average customer total.

question: In 2016, which players out-earned their own team's average salary?
reference_sql: SELECT s.playerID, s.salary FROM Salaries s WHERE s.yearID = 2016 AND s.salary > (SELECT AVG(s2.salary) FROM Salaries s2 WHERE s2.yearID = s.yearID AND s2.teamID = s.teamID)

question: List 2016 players paid above their team average, with the salary.
reference_sql: SELECT s.playerID, s.salary FROM Salaries s WHERE s.yearID = 2016 AND s.salary > (SELECT AVG(s2.salary) FROM Salaries s2 WHERE s2.yearID = s.yearID AND s2.teamID = s.teamID)

question: Who earned more than their team's 2013 average? Show id and salary.
reference_sql: SELECT s.playerID, s.salary FROM Salaries s WHERE s.yearID = 2013 AND s.salary > (SELECT AVG(s2.salary) FROM Salaries s2 WHERE s2.yearID = s.yearID AND s2.teamID = s.teamID)

question: Find 2013 salaries above the same-team average.
reference_sql: SELECT s.playerID, s.salary FROM Salaries s WHERE s.yearID = 2013 AND s.salary > (SELECT AVG(s2.salary) FROM Salaries s2 WHERE s2.yearID = s.yearID AND s2.teamID = s.teamID)

question: Total home runs per player since 2000; who is above the average of those totals?
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT playerID, SUM(HR) AS total FROM Batting WHERE yearID >= 2000 GROUP BY playerID) SELECT playerID, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Which players' career home runs totals since 2000 beat the average player's total?
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT playerID, SUM(HR) AS total FROM Batting WHERE yearID >= 2000 GROUP BY playerID) SELECT playerID, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: First sum home runs per player since 2000, then list players above the cross-player average.
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT playerID, SUM(HR) AS total FROM Batting WHERE yearID >= 2000 GROUP BY playerID) SELECT playerID, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Find players whose summed home runs since 2000 exceeds the average player total.
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT playerID, SUM(HR) AS total FROM Batting WHERE yearID >= 2000 GROUP BY playerID) SELECT playerID, total FROM totals WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Among priced products, which have list price above the average? Show name and price.
reference_sql: SELECT Name, ListPrice FROM Product WHERE ListPrice > 0 AND ListPrice > (SELECT AVG(ListPrice) FROM Product WHERE ListPrice > 0)

question: List name and price where the list price exceeds the priced products average.
reference_sql: SELECT Name, ListPrice FROM Product WHERE ListPrice > 0 AND ListPrice > (SELECT AVG(ListPrice) FROM Product WHERE ListPrice > 0)

reference_sql: SELECT Name, ListPrice FROM Product WHERE ListPrice > 0 AND ListPrice > (SELECT AVG(ListPrice) FROM Product WHERE ListPrice > 0)

question: Among sales orders, which have total due above the average? Show order id and total.

question: Which products cost more than their subcategory's average (over products above 0)?
reference_sql: SELECT p.Name, p.ListPrice FROM Product p WHERE p.ProductSubcategoryID IS NOT NULL AND p.ListPrice > (SELECT AVG(p2.ListPrice) FROM Product p2 WHERE p2.ProductSubcategoryID = p.ProductSubcategoryID AND p2.ListPrice > 0)

question: List products priced above their own subcategory average, counting only prices over 0.
reference_sql: SELECT p.Name, p.ListPrice FROM Product p WHERE p.ProductSubcategoryID IS NOT NULL AND p.ListPrice > (SELECT AVG(p2.ListPrice) FROM Product p2 WHERE p2.ProductSubcategoryID = p.ProductSubcategoryID AND p2.ListPrice > 0)

question: Which customers have a total order value above 10x the average customer total?
reference_sql: WITH totals AS (SELECT h.CustomerID AS ek, SUM(h.TotalDue) AS total FROM SalesOrderHeader h GROUP BY h.CustomerID) SELECT ek, total FROM totals WHERE total > 10 * (SELECT AVG(total) FROM totals)

question: Total the order value per customer; list those beyond 10 times the average.
